name: temperature-curve
title: Temperature Curve
description: The fever curve, with one series per measurement route and antipyretic doses marked.

props.name: data
props.description: `{ time, temperature, route }`. Route is required on every reading.

props.name: antipyretics
props.description: Administrations aligned to the x axis.

props.name: range
props.description: Normal range for the route in use — caller-supplied.

props.name: feverThreshold
props.description: The caller's febrile threshold, drawn as a labelled line.

clinicalNotes: Every reading carries its route, and each route is plotted as its own series. Oral, tympanic, axillary, rectal, and core readings are not interchangeable — an axillary temperature runs roughly half a degree below a core one — so one line through mixed routes manufactures a defervescence out of a change in measurement method. Note the single deliberate exception to this round's no-bridging rule: nulls *within* a route are connected, because there a null means the row was measured by a different route, not that nothing was measured; leaving them broken would shatter each route into isolated dots. A fall after an antipyretic is alignment in time, not proof of effect.
